title: Direct tree-model OOF ensemble
summary: Train separate LightGBM, CatBoost, and XGBoost models on the canonical feature stack and blend their out-of-fold predictions.
rationale: The run used an automated wrapper around tree families, while external competition notebooks show strong tabular ensembles and stacking approaches. A direct implementation gives control over folds, categorical handling, and leakage-safe OOF predictions within the allowed package set.
implementation_hint: Use a fixed grouped or stratified fold scheme, train one LightGBM, one CatBoost, and one XGBoost model with modest parameters and early stopping, collect OOF/test predictions, and test a simple mean plus an OOF-weighted blend. Keep the first run to one seed per model.
expected_effect: Medium AUC gain or stability gain with medium confidence, especially if validation is improved over random holdout.
risk: Runtime can grow with three models but should fit the budget with early stopping. Weighting on a weak validation split can overfit; include simple average as the baseline.
